Aurora Meridian Berhad — Internal Compliance Memorandum re PDPA/CMSA handling for Q2 onboarding, financing, and vendor transfers.
To: Board Risk Committee; From: Dr. Liyana Rahmat, Head of Compliance; Contact: dpo@aurorameridian.my, +60 3-6600 1234.
Listing context: Aurora Meridian Berhad (Main Board, Bayu Securities Exchange) under CMSA insider trading provisions and BSE Listing Requirements overseen by the Malaysian Capital Oversight Commission (MCOC).
Employment onboarding forms collected only role-necessary data (minimisation), and NRIC copies are no longer retained; legacy scans were erased on 15 May 2026 per Retention Schedule 3.
Policy-only note: while the onboarding template mentions fingerprint timekeeping consent, no biometric systems are deployed and no biometric data is collected or stored.
DSAR: Pn. Farah Syafika requested access and correction on 3 May 2026; fulfilled within the statutory period, with redaction of third-party identifiers and confirmation of erasure of recruitment duplicates.
Consent withdrawal: En. Hazriq Naim withdrew consent for optional wellness data on 20 April 2026; processing ceased and his HRIS profile fields were set to null.
Cross-border transfer: Proposed export of payroll attributes to OrbitPay Systems Pte. Ltd. (SG) under Data Export Approval DEA-2026-017; transfer is not active and will not commence until DPA Schedule 2 (Transfer Mechanisms) is countersigned and encryption-at-rest keys are confirmed resident in MY.
For avoidance of doubt, no personal data has been transferred outside Malaysia in Q2; a prior pilot with CloudHarbor APAC Sdn Bhd ran solely on the Kuala Selasih region node.
DPA Schedule 3 (Retention and Minimisation) caps candidate data at 24 months and payroll records at 7 years; purge job PJ-24A completed and logged in the disposal register on 18 May 2026.
Financing: draft term sheet with Pacific Zenith Capital Sdn Bhd includes covenant ratios and pricing triggers; as non-public information likely to affect value, it is treated as MNPI until announcement via the Exchange portal.
Insider controls: the Restricted List is updated and dealing by named insiders paused; the employee share scheme for non-insiders continues under Policy 4.2 without triggering a trading window closure.
Audit: quarterly DLP review shows no outbound attachments to personal emails; seven stale access tokens were revoked, and least-privilege mappings were applied to the JV data room.
Reporting: current privacy workflows do not cross BSE/MCOC notification thresholds; this statement is descriptive and not an indicator of unresolved risk.
For DSARs and erasure requests contact privacy@aurorameridian.my; for MNPI/insider matters contact mnpi.office@aurorameridian.my; vendor approvals via vendor.compliance@aurorameridian.my.
